responses: Welcome to the Maize Disease Monitoring AI Assistant!
Hello! I'm here to help you identify and manage maize diseases.
Hi! Ask me about disease identification, stages, or treatment options.

healthy_maize.description: Maize plant with no disease symptoms
healthy_maize.identification: Leaves are uniformly green, firm, no spots or streaks
healthy_maize.management: Continue regular monitoring and good agronomic practices

common_rust.description: Orange-brown rust pustules on leaf surface
stages: Early: Small (1-3mm) orange pustules on lower leaves
Moderate: Pustules grow (5-10mm), spread to mid-leaves
common_rust.identification: Orange pustules arranged in rows, easily rub off, yellow halo around pustules
common_rust.cause: Fungal disease spread by windborne spores

gray_leaf_spot.description: Rectangular gray lesions on leaf blade
stages: Early: Small (2-3mm) rectangular gray lesions with brown borders
Moderate: Lesions enlarge (5-8mm), spread to mid-leaves
Severe: Large necrotic patches, leaf death, defoliation
gray_leaf_spot.identification: KEY: Rectangular lesion shape (not circular), gray center with reddish-brown border
gray_leaf_spot.cause: Cercospora zeae-maydis fungus, spreads via rain splash and airborne spores

leaf_blight.description: Large irregular necrotic lesions with tissue death
stages: Early: Water-soaked, elongated lesions with yellow halo
Severe: Extensive dead areas, leaf shredding
leaf_blight.identification: Large (1-10cm) irregular lesions, initially water-soaked, black tissue in center
leaf_blight.yield_risk: 20-60% yield loss if ear leaves affected

downy_mildew.description: Yellow striping with fuzzy white growth on leaf undersurface
stages: Early: Light yellow streaks, fuzzy whitish growth below
Moderate: Pronounced yellow/white stripes, dense sporulation
Severe: Severe yellowing, plant stunting, leaf death
downy_mildew.identification: KEY: Yellow striping + WHITE FUZZY GROWTH on leaf underside
downy_mildew.yield_risk: 30-70% with early infection, 10-20% with late infection

maize_streak_virus.description: Yellow streaks running parallel to leaf veins
stages: Early: Thin yellow streaks on emerging leaves
Severe: Necrotic streaks, severe dwarfing, poor ears
maize_streak_virus.identification: KEY: STREAKS parallel to veins (not random), systemic throughout plant
maize_streak_virus.cause: Maize streak virus (MSV) transmitted by leafhoppers

maize_lethal_necrosis.description: Rapid necrosis and plant death syndrome
stages: Critical: Complete leaf death, plant drying, plant death
maize_lethal_necrosis.identification: KEY: RAPID progression (3-5 days), affects young and old leaves, plant drying rapidly
maize_lethal_necrosis.cause: Combination of viruses (SCMV + MNSV or MMV)
maize_lethal_necrosis.progression_time: 3-5 days from Stage 1 to Stage 2, then 2-3 days to death
management: IMMEDIATE: Isolate and remove infected plants

responses: I can help you with: disease identification, disease stages, management strategies, and treatment recommendations.
Ask me about any maize disease and I'll provide detailed information on identification, progression, and management.
